sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 12.46 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.12)

statement.latex: Let$F$be the nonabelian free group on two generators$x,y$. For$a,b { C}$,$|a|=|b|=1$, let$_{a,b}$be the automorphism of${ C}F$defined by$_{a,b}(x)=ax$,$_{a,b}(y)=by$. Given$0 { C}F$, can we always find$a,b { C} \{ 1\}$with$|a|=|b|=1$such that${ C}F _{a,b}( ){ C}F 0$? { P.\,A.\,Linnell} No, not always (A.\,V.\,Tushev, Ukrain. Mat.~J., { 47}, no.\,4 (1995), 571--572).

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
